- `GET /health` (line 7)
- `GET /users` (line 11) — returns a hardcoded list of users, no `:id` parameter

A request like `GET /users/123` against the current code would not crash the server — Express would simply return a 404 because no matching route is registered. There's no `req.params.id` access anywhere that could throw a `TypeError`.
